_schema: rotation_history v1 — one snapshot per close (the ~20:00 UTC / 10:30 PM Kyiv scan). Flows in USD millions (1200 = $1.2B, 0.15 = $150K). dir: in=inflow post, out=outflow post. tier: core|sub. signal: BUYING THE DIP | SELLING INTO STRENGTH | null. new=NEW INFLOW/OUTFLOW flag. Append one object per trading day; the dashboard renders the growing timeline. The optional 'tags' object ({selling_into_strength, buying_the_dip, of}) is our own tally of how many sector cards carried each signal tag in the feed — an observed count, NOT the bot's regime-banner 'selling' figure (which counts a broader universe and is only set when that banner is captured).
_rules: Only confirmed Rotation Radar Bot feed numbers. Append-only. Never invent or carry-forward. Omit a flow field when the feed didn't show it. The optional 'read' field is editorial analyst commentary (headline + points[{t,d}] + watch) rendered as the 'Analyst Read' panel — interpretation only, every number it cites must come from the feed data above it.

read.headline: Flat tape, heavy distribution underneath — money rotated out of leaders into beaten-down cyclicals.

points.t: Tech, semis & financials sold into strength
points.d: SMH drew +$180.9M on the day but bled −$632.5M over 5 sessions (SELLING INTO STRENGTH); XLF leaked −$326.4M (−$380.8M/5d), XLE −$424.6M/5d, XLV −$308.1M/5d, XLC −$191.4M/5d — all red on flow while SPY closed +0.24%.

points.t: Cyclicals & staples bought the dip
points.d: Six core 'down-on-price' sectors were flagged BUYING THE DIP: XLY pulled +$695.1M over 5 days, XLP +$164.2M, XOP +$136.4M, XRT +$87.8M, XLRE +$80.7M, XLI +$199.6M.

read.watch: SMH's −$632.5M 5-day flow turning deeper, or XLF outflow extending, would confirm rotation out of 2026's leaders. 14-day conviction already has XLK and XLE in sustained institutional distribution ($2.0B and $4.2B).

history.holdNote: The 'holding up' names are from the 13:30 UTC broad-market read (the only sell-off summary posted Jun 1); selling/total (21/26) are from that same post. Flows, conviction and benchmark are the 20:01 UTC close.

read.headline: A green tape hiding a quiet exit — distribution was already running two sessions before the break.

points.t: The strongest names were the ones being sold
points.d: SMH carried a −$1.2B 5-day outflow flagged ⚠️ Selling into Strength even while up +4.01% on the day and +24.75% over 20 sessions. IAI showed the same: green-ish tape, −$106.8M out.

points.t: Financials and energy leaked under a flat index
points.d: XLF bled −$380.8M and XLE −$424.6M (5-day) while SPY sat green on every horizon (+0.14% / +1.20% / +5.79%). Distribution with no price warning attached.

points.t: Money rotated toward value and defensives
points.d: XLY +$695.1M, XLI +$199.6M, XLP +$164.2M and XRT +$87.8M all printed 💰 Buying the Dip — institutions rotating into laggards while trimming the leaders.

read.watch: A market near highs on flat breadth while its biggest sectors (financials, semis) bleed institutional money is late-cycle behavior — when flow diverges from price, price usually follows.

read.headline: The sell-off's fault lines were already drawn — money was leaving financials and defensives while price was still flat.

points.t: Financials distribution was already a 14-day pattern
points.d: XLF showed a −$919.5M 5-day outflow at the close while finishing only −1.04% on the day, and the conviction board flagged it 5/14 days 'Selling into Strength' worth +$3.1B. The exit was well underway before the tape cracked.

points.t: The defensives 'holding up' were the ones being sold
points.d: XLV bled −$1.3B (5-day) while closing GREEN (+0.77%); XLP leaked −$132.7M and XLU printed 💰 BUYING THE DIP despite a −3.07% 5-day. Strength used as an exit — classic distribution.

points.t: Semis absorbed the hit
points.d: SMH pulled +$1.3B of inflow on the day (+$661.3M 5-day) and was one of only four names 'Holding Up' at midday (+1.51%), alongside DRAM (+0.52%). The buy-the-semis-crash thesis started here.

read.watch: A defensive that's green on price but red on 5-day flow (XLV, XLP) is where the next leg down starts; XLF's outflow breaking past −$1B would confirm the rotation has further to run.

read.headline: Price flipped, money didn't — this is a rotation, not a liquidation.

points.t: Semis & tech bought through the crash
points.d: SMH drew ~$770M of 5-day inflow on BOTH the up day (Jun 4, SPY +0.42%) and the −9% crash (Jun 5). XLK inflow grew +$63M → +$190M, XME +$65M → +$122M. The harder they fell, the more money came in — conviction, not panic.

points.t: Financials & defensives sold into strength
points.d: XLF leaked −$1.1B → −$1.4B while still green on price; XLP −$440M → −$644M, plus XLV, XLE and IAI (9 of 14 days) all net sellers both days.

read.watch: SMH 5-day flow turning negative would break the dip-buy thesis; XLF outflow past −$1.4B deepens the rotation. A 'holding-up' defensive that's already red on flow is where the next leg down starts.

history.holdNote: The sectors "Holding Up" on Jun 5 are the exact sectors being quietly sold. XLF (+0.21% on the tape) leaked −$1.4B. XLV, XLP, XLU — all green on price, all net outflows. Classic late-cycle distribution into strength: the defensives look safe on the screen while institutions use that strength to exit. Meanwhile the scary-looking semis crash is where smart money is adding.

read.headline: Flat tape, violent rotation — strength sold, weakness bought.

points.t: Core sectors distributed into a green open
points.d: SPY closed +0.26% but the heavyweight sectors were net sellers over 5 days — XLF −$566.8M, XLE −$483.5M, XLY −$408.7M, XRT −$313.1M, XLP −$249.2M — every one tagged SELLING INTO STRENGTH. The flat index masked a steady exit from financials, energy and discretionary.

points.t: Tech & semis bought the dip hard
points.d: XLK flipped to a NEW OUTFLOW on price (−5.87% 5D) yet drew +$249.8M of 1-day inflow and +$288.0M over 5 days — active dip-buying. SMH led the tape at +5.00% on +$526.7M of 5-day inflow; XME (+$24.4M, BUYING THE DIP) and IGV (+$186.6M) show the same pattern. Money is rotating from the old leaders into beaten-down growth.

read.watch: IAI is the most persistent seller — 10/14 days SELLING INTO STRENGTH, +$1.1B cumulative; XLF distribution is now +$6.0B over 14 days. If SMH/XLK 5-day inflows reverse to net outflow, the dip-buy thesis breaks. MSOS +12.92%, DRAM +8.48% and BITO are momentum outliers, not rotation signals.

history.holdNote: The tape was nearly flat (SPY +0.26%) but the rotation underneath was violent. Semis and tech led on both price and flow — SMH +5.00% (+$526.7M 5-day inflow), XLK dip-bought (+$249.8M 1-day) despite a 5-day NEW OUTFLOW. Meanwhile financials, energy and defensives were sold into the open: XLF −$566.8M, XLE −$483.5M, XLP −$249.2M — all flat-to-green on price, all net sellers (SELLING INTO STRENGTH). Smart money is leaving last cycle's leaders and accumulating beaten-down growth. MSOS, DRAM and BITO are momentum outliers, not part of the institutional rotation.

read.headline: Green tape, red flows — broad distribution into strength, two-way dip-buying in beaten-down tech.

points.t: Defensives & financials sold into the rally
points.d: XLP closed +1.29% but bled -$693.8M over 5 days; XLF +0.91% on price yet -$414.0M 5D; XLY, XLE, XRT, XLC and XLRE all green-to-flat and tagged SELLING INTO STRENGTH. IAI leads the distribution at 10/14 days (+$1.1B), with XLF (7/14, +$5.9B) and XLY (7/14, +$2.0B) right behind.

points.t: Crushed tech & miners drew dip-buyers
points.d: XME (-12.55% 5D) pulled +$103.6M of 5-day inflow — BUYING THE DIP. IGV (-11.20% 5D) drew +$93.8M and ICLN +$85.4M, both BUYING THE DIP. COPX BUYING THE DIP (+$66.7M 5D) on a -14.50% slide. The conviction-accumulation board (XLC, COPX, XLP, IGV, ICLN) is buying weakness while the index rallies.

read.watch: XLF/XLY distribution streaks (7/14 days) deepening would confirm institutions are exiting the rally. Sustained BUYING THE DIP in IGV/COPX/ICLN against their price downtrends is the two-way tell — if that flow reverses, the dip-buy thesis breaks. SMH flipped to NEW OUTFLOW (-$224.1M 5D) — watch whether semis lose their bid.

read.headline: Index red across every horizon, but flows split two ways — heavy distribution into the green-tape defensives and financials, dip-buying in crushed tech and clean energy.

points.t: Sectors that closed green were the ones being sold
points.d: XLP finished +1.69% on the day yet bled -$411.7M over 5 sessions; XLE +1.58% but -$548.4M/5D; XRT, XOP, XLC and XLRE all flat-to-green and tagged SELLING INTO STRENGTH. The conviction board has IAI leading distribution at 10/14 days (+$1.1B), XLY 8/14 (+$2.1B) and XLF 6/14 (+$4.7B).

points.t: Beaten-down tech and clean energy drew dip-buyers
points.d: XLK (-9.81% 5D) pulled +$499.0M of 5-day inflow flagged BUYING THE DIP, with XLB (-3.83% 5D) +$466.4M alongside it. IGV (-8.49% 5D) drew +$93.8M, ICLN (-13.99% 5D) +$85.4M and COPX (-14.13% 5D) +$66.7M, all BUYING THE DIP — the accumulation board (IGV, ICLN, COPX, XLC, XLP) is buying weakness while the index falls.

read.watch: SMH flipped to NEW OUTFLOW — +$917.6M on the day against a -$650.2M 5-day, semis sold into the bounce. If the XLF/XLY/IAI distribution streaks keep extending while the IGV/COPX/ICLN dip-buys reverse, the two-way support underneath this tape breaks.

read.headline: Broad market lifted on thin flow while the real buying stayed narrow — tech & semis were bought, everything else got carried.

points.t: The genuine demand was concentrated
points.d: SMH drew +$696.0M on the day (BUYING THE DIP) and XLK pulled +$861.6M over 5 sessions; 14-day conviction shows sustained accumulation only in IGV (+$657.0M), ICLN (+$597.8M) and COPX (+$400.1M). That's where money actually moved.

points.t: The broad rally ran on trivial net flow
points.d: Core sectors rose 1–4% on tiny flows and were tagged SELLING INTO STRENGTH: XLC +1.05% on −$153.4M/5d, XRT +3.08% on −$103.3M, XHB +4.15% on −$69.1M, plus XLV and XLRE — all green-on-price, red-on-flow. Price lifted on thin participation, not buying.

points.t: Smart money kept distributing underneath
points.d: 14-day distribution stays heavy: IAI 10/14 days selling into strength (+$1.1B), XLF +$4.1B, XLP +$2.1B, XLE and XLY +$2.0B each, XLK +$1.6B. XLE also bled −$637.1M over 5 days.

read.watch: Whether the narrow tech/semis buying broadens out, or the thin broad-market lift fades back toward 5-day flows that are still net negative (SPY −2.48%/5d). A low-participation melt-up has no buyer base underneath if momentum stalls.

read.headline: A broad green close masking textbook rotation: fresh money chased beaten-down miners and commodities while the crowded financials-and-staples winners were quietly distributed.

points.t: New money rotated into the laggards
points.d: COPX (+3.46%), REMX (+2.71%), LIT (+2.09%) and OIH (+0.56%) all printed NEW INFLOW despite deep 20-day holes (−2.98% to −5.20%); COPX drew +$66.7M over 5 days. Even BITO flagged a NEW INFLOW off a −22.17%/20d base. The fresh demand showed up in beaten-down materials, miners and commodities — not the leaders.

points.t: The day's green came with red flow on the crowded winners
points.d: XME (+1.73%), XLY (+0.26%), XRT (+0.07%) and IAI (+1.87%) all rose on the day yet were tagged SELLING INTO STRENGTH on negative flow (XLY −$132.2M/1d, XME −$238.8M/5d, IAI −$106.8M/5d). Financials led price — XLF +1.30%, KRE +1.51%, KBE +1.45% — but XLF still bled −$496.6M on the day.

points.t: Software was the one dip that got bought
points.d: IGV slipped to a NEW OUTFLOW on price (−5.31%/5d) yet pulled +$186.6M/1d tagged BUYING THE DIP, alongside XLV (+$77.0M) and ICLN (+$85.4M/5d). 14-day conviction shows sustained accumulation only in IGV (+$750.8M), ICLN (+$683.2M) and COPX (+$333.4M); distribution stayed far broader — IAI 10/14d (+$1.1B), XLY and XLE +$2.0B each, XLP +$2.1B, XRT +$1.3B.

read.watch: Whether the new inflows into miners and commodities (COPX, REMX, LIT, OIH) build into durable leadership, or fade like the cloud/cyber/cannabis names (WCLD, HACK, MSOS) that just flipped to NEW OUTFLOW. SPY is still −0.89%/20d, so the broad lift hasn't repaired the longer trend.

read.headline: Close scan: a broad green tape (SPY +1.66%) over textbook rotation — the crowded winners (staples, discretionary, retail, materials) sold into strength while fresh money chased miners, semis and alt-assets.

points.t: Distribution into the crowded winners held into the close
points.d: XLP (−$420.5M/5d), XLY (−$91.5M/5d), XRT (−$79.0M/5d), XLB (−$57.2M/5d), XME (−$257.1M/5d) and IAI (−$106.8M/5d) all carried negative 5-day flow with SELLING INTO STRENGTH tags. 14-day conviction confirms the persistence: IAI 11/14d (+$1.2B), XLY 7/14d (+$1.9B), XRT 7/14d (+$1.4B), XLP 6/14d (+$2.5B), XLE 6/14d (+$2.0B).

points.t: Fresh money rotated into laggards and alt-assets
points.d: GDX (+6.50%/1d), URA (+5.49%), LIT (+9.09%/5d) and BITO (+4.68%) all flagged NEW INFLOW off negative/shallow 20-day reads, joined by REMX. XOP (−9.07%/20d) drew BUYING THE DIP (+$100.0M/5d), as did software IGV (+$186.6M/1d) and clean-energy ICLN (+$85.4M/5d). Sustained 14-day accumulation sits only in ICLN 9/14d (+$768.6M), IGV 8/14d (+$750.8M) and COPX 5/14d (+$333.4M).

points.t: Defensives and momentum names flipped to NEW OUTFLOW
points.d: XLV (Healthcare, −$98.2M/5d), XHE (Healthcare Equipment), HACK (+9.68%/20d), WCLD (+7.78%/20d) and MSOS (+11.79%/20d) all printed NEW OUTFLOW — defensive healthcare plus the cyber/cloud/cannabis momentum trades are being sold. Meanwhile tech and semis led price: XLK +3.71%/1d, SMH +4.38%/1d (+$1.1B/5d), DRAM +9.41%/1d (+39.20%/20d).

read.watch: Whether the NEW OUTFLOWs in healthcare, cyber, cloud and cannabis mark momentum rolling over, or just profit-taking inside an uptrend. The dip-buying in energy/miners (XOP, COPX, GDX, URA) vs. distribution in staples/discretionary is the cleanest rotation tell on the board. SPY closed +2.01%/20d — the broad trend has now turned up; watch which side builds next week.

read.headline: Down day, uptrend intact (SPY −0.61%/1d but +1.57%/20d) over a textbook rotation — the crowded winners sold into strength while fresh money bought the tech/semis dip and chased miners, uranium and crypto.

points.t: Tech & semis sold on price but drew dip-buying
points.d: XLK −2.69%/1d, SMH −4.81%/1d and DRAM −4.22%/1d led the tape lower, yet SMH still pulled +$1.6B/5d of inflow and XLK +$94.8M/5d — the leadership fell in price but money bought the dip rather than fleeing.

points.t: Distribution into the crowded winners
points.d: XME (−$245.3M/5d), XLY (−$62.4M/5d) and IAI (−$106.8M/5d) carried SELLING INTO STRENGTH. 14-day conviction confirms the persistence: IAI 12/14d (+$1.3B), XLY 7/14d (+$1.6B), XRT 7/14d (+$1.4B), XLP 6/14d (+$2.5B), XLE 6/14d (+$2.0B), XLRE 5/14d (+$521.2M).

points.t: Dip-buying in energy, miners and laggards
points.d: XLE (+$311.8M/5d) and XOP (+$307.6M/5d) drew BUYING THE DIP off deep 20-day losses (−8.72%, −10.83%), joined by KRE (+$396.5M/5d), IGV (+$93.8M/5d) and ICLN (+$85.4M/5d). GDX, URA, LIT and BITO all flagged NEW INFLOW; sustained 14-day accumulation sits only in ICLN 10/14d (+$854.0M), IGV 8/14d (+$750.8M) and COPX 5/14d (+$333.4M).

points.t: Defensives and momentum names flipped to NEW OUTFLOW
points.d: XLV (Healthcare) and XLRE (Real Estate) flipped to NEW OUTFLOW on the core side — though both still drew dip-buying (+$207.8M, +$144.0M/5d) — while WCLD (Cloud) and MSOS (Cannabis) printed NEW OUTFLOW among sub-sectors.

read.watch: Whether the price drop in tech/semis (SMH −4.81%/1d, DRAM −4.22%) marks leadership rolling over or just a dip being bought (SMH +$1.6B/5d inflow argues the latter). The cleanest rotation tell is dip-buying in energy/miners (XLE, XOP, GDX, URA) against distribution in the crowded winners (XME, XLY, IAI). SPY held +1.57%/20d — the broad trend is still up.

read.headline: Broad-based sell-off (32/40 sectors selling, SPY −1.30%/1d) into a Hindenburg trigger — only AI/semis leadership held while distribution hit the crowded defensives and financials and dip-buying broadened across the beaten-down.

points.t: Only semis/AI leadership held up
points.d: With 80% of sectors selling, the green list was narrow: SMH (+1.29%/1d, +$1.1B/5d inflow), XBI (+2.95%/1d, +$289.8M/5d), DRAM (+21.91%/5d) and XAR (+1.23%/1d). Even XLK held its price drop to −0.43% but carried SELLING INTO STRENGTH (−$124.3M/5d). SMH's +$1.1B/5d is the last big inflow standing — if it cracks there's little underneath.

points.t: Sustained distribution in defensives & financials
points.d: 14-day conviction stayed distribution-heavy: IAI 13/14d (+$1.4B), XRT 7/14d (+$1.4B), XLP 6/14d (+$2.5B), XLE 6/14d (+$2.0B), XLY 6/14d (+$1.3B), XLRE 5/14d (+$521.2M). XLP (−$133.0M/5d) and XLY (−$91.5M/5d) carried negative 5-day flow.

points.t: Dip-buying broadened across the wreckage
points.d: BUYING THE DIP tags spread to XOP (+$242.8M/5d), XLV (+$184.9M), XLU (+$123.8M), XLRE (+$105.8M) and XRT (+$161.5M) on the core side, plus KRE (+$715.5M/5d), IGV (+$93.8M) and KBE (+$56.5M) among sub-sectors. KRE's inflow was the largest dip-buy on the board.

points.t: Fresh rotation flips: NEW OUTFLOW vs NEW INFLOW
points.d: NEW OUTFLOW hit XLV, XRT, XTN (core) and KRE, KBE, MSOS (sub) — defensives, financials and transports flipping to outflow. NEW INFLOW flagged ICLN, URA, BOTZ and BITO — alt-energy, uranium, robotics and crypto drawing fresh money off depressed 20-day reads.

read.watch: Whether the 32/40 broad sell-off plus the Hindenburg trigger marks a genuine top or a one-day flush — SPY still held +0.93%/20d, barely. Semis/AI (SMH +$1.1B/5d) is the only leadership left; watch if it follows the tape down. The cleanest tell is dip-buying in financials/energy (KRE, XOP, XLE) against sustained distribution in staples and broker-dealers (XLP, IAI).

read.headline: Risk-on tape led by tech/semis (SPY +0.95%/1d) — XLK (+$9.0B/1d) and SMH (+$7.1B/1d) dwarf every other flow, while institutions keep distributing defensives/financials and dip-buying broadens across the beaten-down.

points.t: Tech & semis are the whole inflow story
points.d: XLK led core inflows (+3.22%/1d, +$9.0B/1d, +$8.6B/5d) and SMH led the sub-sectors (+5.76%/1d, +$7.1B/1d, +$7.5B/5d). DRAM ran +10.12%/1d (+49.54%/20d) on the memory theme. Inflows are overwhelmingly concentrated in semis/AI.

points.t: Sustained distribution in defensives & financials
points.d: 14-day conviction stayed distribution-heavy: IAI a perfect 14/14d Selling into Strength (+$1.5B), XLP 6/14d (+$2.5B), XLE 6/14d (+$2.0B), XLY 6/14d (+$1.3B), XRT 7/14d (+$1.4B). IAI's own 5-day flow turned negative (-$106.8M).

points.t: Dip-buying broadened across the wreckage
points.d: BUYING THE DIP tags spread to XAR (+$809.1M/5d), XLB (+$279.7M), XRT (+$144.8M), XOP (+$133.8M), XLRE (+$107.7M) and XLV (+$63.1M), plus KRE (+$254.7M) and IGV (+$93.8M) — accumulation into the sectors that sold off.

read.watch: Energy is the clear laggard — XLE -1.75%/1d, -10.18%/20d with -$170.5M/1d outflow; XOP -12.23%/20d; OIH -9.57%/5d, -14.62%/20d. Breadth is narrow: with semis/AI carrying the tape, watch whether the SMH/XLK inflow holds — if it cracks there's little broad participation underneath.

read.headline: Distribution-led morning tape — institutions keep selling defensives & financials (IAI a perfect 14/14d Selling into Strength, +$1.5B) while broad dip-buying (XLF +$870.4M/5d, XAR +$809.1M/5d) cushions the beaten-down. Semis stay the lone runaway: SMH +$7.5B/5d (+17.62%/20d).

points.t: Semis are still the whole inflow story
points.d: SMH led the sub-sector inflows: +1.22%/1d, +7.74%/5d, +17.62%/20d with +$7.5B/5d of measured flow — dwarfing every other inflow. DRAM (Memory) ran +4.52%/1d, +23.33%/5d, +47.55%/20d on the memory theme. Outside semis/AI, sub-sector flows were modest and mixed.

points.t: Sustained distribution in financials & defensives
points.d: 14-day conviction stayed distribution-heavy: IAI a perfect 14/14d Selling into Strength (+$1.5B), XLP 6/14d (+$2.5B), XLE 6/14d (+$2.0B), XLY 6/14d (+$1.3B), XRT 7/14d (+$1.4B). IAI's own 5-day flow is negative (-$106.8M) even as price holds (+2.17%/5d).

points.t: Dip-buying is broad across the wreckage
points.d: BUYING THE DIP tagged 11 sector cards: XLF (+$870.4M/5d), XAR (+$809.1M), XLB (+$362.2M), XOP (+$277.7M), XRT (+$248.0M), KRE (+$157.5M), XLY (+$105.1M), IGV (+$93.8M), XLRE (+$67.2M), COPX (+$66.7M) and XME (+$60.0M) — accumulation into the sectors that sold off.

read.watch: Energy stays the clear laggard — XLE -7.26%/5d, -9.74%/20d; XOP -7.52%/5d, -10.40%/20d; OIH -10.89%/5d, -14.28%/20d — even as all three drew positive 5-day dip-buying flow. With SMH (+$7.5B/5d) dwarfing every other measured inflow, breadth is narrow: watch whether the semis bid holds, because little else is pulling sustained inflow.

read.headline: Tech-led sell-off — SPY −1.31% with Technology −3.32% the worst sector on a −$9.3B one-day outflow from XLK (semis −$7.3B). Money rotated to safety on price (Consumer Defensive +1.79%, Real Estate +1.34%, Healthcare +1.31%), but 5-day flows tell a subtler story: defensives were SELLING INTO STRENGTH while institutions kept BUYING THE DIP in beaten-down materials, semis and software.

points.t: Tech took the hit
points.d: XLK led the tape lower — −4.01%/1d on a −$9.3B one-day outflow, flipping to NEW OUTFLOW (−$947.4M/5d). Semis (SMH) fell −7.01%/1d with a −$7.3B 1-day outflow but a positive +$326.4M/5d — tagged BUYING THE DIP.

points.t: Defensives rose on price, but saw net selling
points.d: Consumer Defensive +1.79%, Real Estate +1.34% and Healthcare +1.31% led the heat map, yet XLV, XLP, XLU, XLE and XLF were all tagged SELLING INTO STRENGTH — 5-day flows negative (XLP −$612.8M, XLV −$698.4M) even as price held. A defensive bid, not conviction buying.

points.t: Dip-buying in cyclicals & materials
points.d: BUYING THE DIP across XLB (+$357.9M/5d), XAR, XME, SMH and IGV; XLI (+$782.1M/5d) and XBI (+$469.8M) drew the largest core inflows. 14-day conviction stays split — IGV a near-perfect 13/14d accumulation (+$1.2B) vs IAI 14/14d distribution (+$1.5B).

read.watch: Energy flipped to NEW INFLOW while Tech and Semis flipped to NEW OUTFLOW — an early rotation tell. Watch whether the dip-buying in semis/materials holds or the tech outflow broadens; sustained distribution in financials (XLF 5/14d, +$3.7B) and staples (XLP 7/14d, +$3.1B) is the persistent overhang.

read.headline: Price bounced, flow didn't follow. SPY −0.16%/1d but −2.14%/5d, and the rally was broad on the tape — Homebuilders +5.52%, Airlines +4.15%, Retail +2.76%, Biotech +1.75% — while 5-day institutional flow stayed in heavy distribution across the megacap core: Technology −$762M, Consumer Staples −$490M, Healthcare −$463M and Financials −$373M all tagged SELLING INTO STRENGTH. The genuine accumulation is in the beaten-down corners: semis, biotech, materials, miners and software.

points.t: Defensives & financials: rising price, exiting money
points.d: XLK, XLP, XLV, XLF, XHB, XLU and KBE all rose or held on the day yet carry negative 5-day flow and a SELLING INTO STRENGTH tag — institutions feeding the bounce, not chasing it. Broker-Dealers (IAI) flipped to NEW OUTFLOW (−$106.8M/5d) and lead 14-day distribution at 13/14 days.

points.t: Real buying is in the wreckage
points.d: SMH semis +$760.8M/5d and XBI biotech +$867.7M/5d drew the largest inflows, while down-tape Materials (XLB), Metals (XME), Aerospace (XAR), Software (IGV) and Copper (COPX) were tagged BUYING THE DIP. IGV leads 14-day conviction at a perfect 14/14 days (+$1.3B).

points.t: Commodities & speculative names still bleeding
points.d: Energy/metals complex stayed weak on 5-day flow — XLE −$446.6M, GDX, OIH, SLX, URA, LIT all negative — and the risk tail (BITO −8.74%/5d, MSOS −8.59%, BOTZ, WCLD) kept sliding. The dip-buying is selective, not a broad risk-on.

read.watch: Whether the SELLING INTO STRENGTH core (Tech, Staples, Healthcare, Financials) sees 5-day flow turn positive — that would confirm the price bounce. Until then it's distribution into strength: a warning, not a green light.
